title: [Bug]: GitHub CI fails on connector entry-point sort lambda
summary: The GitHub CI run on the v0.4.1 main merge failed because the hosted runner enabled Ruff rule PLW0108 and flagged an unnecessary lambda in the sink connector entry-point sort key.
observed: The release workflow for v0.4.1 passed, but the separate main-branch CI run failed during `ruff check .` with `PLW0108 Lambda may be unnecessary` in `src/nats_sinks/sinks/connectors.py`.
expected: The connector registry code should pass Ruff on GitHub-hosted runners and locally, even when Ruff enables PLW0108. The sort key should remain deterministic and behavior-preserving.
reproduction: Run `ruff check src/nats_sinks/sinks/connectors.py --select PLW0108` against the released v0.4.1 main commit.
failing_test: Use the GitHub CI lint result and a focused Ruff command as the failing regression signal. The standard CI lint step already exercises this path.
impact: Low runtime severity. The issue does not affect NATS delivery, ACK order, sink persistence, encryption, idempotency, or payload handling. It does create a failed main CI signal immediately after release, which can confuse release health checks.
delivery_semantics: No delivery semantics are affected. The fix must not change connector loading, connector allow-listing, entry-point validation, NATS behavior, Oracle behavior, Oracle MySQL behavior, file sink behavior, or ACK timing.
security: The fix must preserve the existing fail-closed external connector allow-list and must not load unapproved entry points. No secrets, endpoints, credentials, payloads, or environment-specific values are required for reproduction or verification.
acceptance: The connector entry-point sort remains deterministic.
The standard local release check passes before the fix is marked completed.
CHANGELOG.md records the CI lint compatibility fix.
tests: Run `ruff check src/nats_sinks/sinks/connectors.py --select PLW0108`, focused connector unit tests, bug-report validation, and `scripts/check.sh`.
documentation: Update CHANGELOG.md under Unreleased. No user-facing runtime documentation is needed because behavior does not change.
closeout: Close after the release containing the fix is published. Include the release tag, focused Ruff evidence, full check evidence, and confirmation that connector loading behavior did not change.
